articleTitle: Toward actionable interventions in human aging (12th ARDD meeting, 2025).
takeaways: The field of aging research has shifted from simply describing aging processes to understanding the specific molecular mechanisms that can be targeted for intervention, suggesting that aging may be a reversible biological process rather than inevitable damage accumulation

Artificial intelligence and machine learning platforms are now being integrated with biological research to accelerate the identification of anti-aging drug targets and speed up therapeutic development timelines
Researchers are developing improved preclinical models that better translate findings from laboratory studies to successful human clinical trials for longevity interventions
The focus has evolved from cataloging the established hallmarks of aging to identifying and modulating the specific molecular pathways that regulate these aging processes
Personalized therapeutic interventions for extending healthspan are becoming feasible through the convergence of multiple scientific disciplines including biology, AI, and drug development
